title: Credential 'Allowed domains' wildcard bypass enables credentialed requests to attacker domains in n8n
description: n8n's isDomainAllowed in packages/workflow/src/utils.ts enforces the HTTP Request credential's 'Allowed domains' allowlist, including wildcard entries like *.example.com. For wildcard entries it computed domainSuffix = 'example.com' and accepted the request URL when hostname.endsWith(domainSuffix) was true. Because the leading dot is not required, a hostname such as evilexample.com or attacker-example.com also ends with 'example.com' and is wrongly treated as allowed. An authenticated user configuring an HTTP Request node can thus point a credentialed request at an attacker-controlled domain that merely shares the suffix, causing the server to send the credential to an unintended destination (credential exfiltration via an allowlist/SSRF-protection bypass).
